title: viguieranthus subauriculatus
intro: Viguieranthus subauriculatus, also known as the shrubby guinea flower or the subauriculatus, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Madagascar and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Viguieranthus subauriculatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable plant in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Viguieranthus subauriculatus are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
